Children.Text: VeloxDev provides .NET developers with the complete architecture needed to build interactive workflow editors.

### Adapter Library Out-of-the-box, bound to GUI framework. If you need agent and serialization capabilities, you can additionally install VeloxDev.Core.Extension.

| Platform | Package | NuGet |
|----------|---------|-------|
| WPF | `VeloxDev.WPF` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WPF?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WPF/) |
| Avalonia | `VeloxDev.Avalonia` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Avalonia?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Avalonia/) |
| WinUI | `VeloxDev.WinUI` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WinUI?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WinUI/) |
| MAUI | `VeloxDev.MAUI` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.MAUI?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.MAUI/) |
| WinForms | `VeloxDev.WinForms` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WinForms?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WinForms/) |
| Razor | `VeloxDev.Razor` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Razor?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Razor/) |

###  Core Library

> Independent of the GUI framework, optional; their updates may be faster than the adaptation libraries, and sometimes need to be installed/upgraded separately.

| Package | NuGet | Description |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| `VeloxDev.Core` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Core?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Core/) | Workflow abstractions, MVVM generators, and runtime models — zero third-party dependencies |
| `VeloxDev.Core.Extension` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Core.Extension?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Core.Extension/) | MAF-based Workflow Agent tools and additional runtime extensions |
Browsing this chapter, you will quickly understand important historical version updates. The document pruning rules are as follows:

Cells: Modify the usage of existing feature blocks
Create a subpage in the [Version] section to explain the new content, and mark the version of change in the corresponding original text.
Add directly in the original text

Children.Text: Summary: Agent building paradigm upgrade & increase in branch node creation pathways

The agent can now initiate permission confirmation dialogs and radio buttons. This is an important milestone, marking that you can orchestrate agent tasks in a more controlled manner. Additionally, the workspace now includes a new set of practical configuration methods, which encompass: global language configuration and automatic scanning of workflow context from assemblies.
First, a new interface definition: when you want to use a custom type to create a branch with multiple connectors, you can implement the ISlotProvider interface.
Thus, you can easily implement a conditional provider that presents multiple connectors at once.

Finally, when you want to have custom conditional routing within the Node component, you can use the previously defined CustomRouteSelector to dynamically generate it. Once defined, the agent will understand the construction of CustomRouteSelector based on serialization, and based on the creation and passing of instance objects, realize dynamic connector generation.
Summary: Item template support, SlotEnumerator improvements, MAUI attached property improvements
Summary: Prompt optimization, upgrade of aspect-oriented programming approach, removal of outdated AOT implementation (.NET built-in mechanism is the best)

The source generator no longer generates a property named Proxy for the view model. Instead, while retaining the dynamic creation of the original interface, it adds extension methods and global cache management. This is to completely eliminate the potential risk of inheritance chain disruption. The API has undergone major changes.
The IProxy interface has been renamed to avoid ambiguity with concepts such as network proxies.
Built-in weak reference dictionary, when accessing the proxy via `Aop()`, queries the dictionary cache, and automatically constructs it if it does not exist.
In rare cases, you may need to reverse-lookup the proxy target of a proxy object; the return value can be null.

# Channel-level AsyncLoop Override Mechanism **Related file:** `Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core/TimeLine/MonoBehaviourManager.cs`
> **Changes:** +49 / -3 lines

---

## Background

Previously, `MonoBehaviourManager` had only one global static property `UseAsyncLoop` that controls the frame loop mode of **all channels**. If you need to use async/await for a specific channel while other channels continue to use native Thread, it is not achievable.

## Design Improvements

Introducing the mechanism of **independent override per channel**, add a nullable field as an override value, falling back to the global setting when `null`.

### New Field

```csharp
private bool? _useAsyncLoopOverride;
```

Add Computed Property

```csharp
private bool EffectiveUseAsyncLoop => _useAsyncLoopOverride ?? MonoBehaviourManager.UseAsyncLoop;
```

The judgment of all frame loop start points changed from `MonoBehaviourManager.UseAsyncLoop` to `EffectiveUseAsyncLoop`.

### Instance Methods (Channel Level)

```csharp
/// <summary>
/// Sets whether the current channel uses async/await instead of native Thread-driven frame loop.
/// </summary>
/// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">Throws an exception if the channel has already started.</exception>
public void SetUseAsyncLoop(bool useAsyncLoop);

/// <summary>
/// Clears the current channel's individual override configuration and falls back to the global UseAsyncLoop.
/// </summary>
/// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">Throws an exception if the channel has already started.</exception>
public void ClearUseAsyncLoopOverride();
```

### Static API (Delegated to Channel)

```csharp
// Set by channel name
public static void SetUseAsyncLoop(bool useAsyncLoop, string channel = DEFAULT_CHANNEL);

// Clear by channel name
public static void ClearUseAsyncLoopOverride(string channel = DEFAULT_CHANNEL);
```

---

## Call Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[调用 SetUseAsyncLoop/bool] --> B{通道已启动?}
    B -->|是| C[抛出 InvalidOperationException]
    B -->|否| D[设置 _useAsyncLoopOverride]
    E[帧循环启动] --> F{EffectiveUseAsyncLoop}
    F --> G{_useAsyncLoopOverride 有值?}
    G -->|是| H[使用通道级覆盖值]
    G -->|null| I[回退到全局 UseAsyncLoop]
```

---

## Design Highlights

- **Channel Isolation**: Each `Channel` instance independently maintains its own `_useAsyncLoopOverride`
- **Safety guard**: During channel operation, modifying the override value is prohibited to prevent switching the drive mode in the middle of a frame cycle.
- **Transparent fallback**: `null` means "not covered", automatically uses global `UseAsyncLoop`
- **Modifiable after stop**: After the channel stops, you can freely modify the override value, and it will take effect upon restart.

---

## Related Tests

Add complete unit test coverage:

| Test Category | Test Case | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SetUseAsyncLoop | [View test details](https://github.com/Axvser/VeloxDev/blob/master/Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core.Test/TimeLine/MonoBehaviourManagerTests.cs) | Override settings before/after start and after stop |
| ClearUseAsyncLoopOverride | Same as above | Various scenarios for clearing overrides |
| Channel isolation | Same as above | Different channels do not affect each other | Test file: `Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core.Test/TimeLine/MonoBehaviourManagerTests.cs` (new, untracked)

---

## Rollback Guide

If you want to keep the global behavior of v5.4.0, no changes are needed — all channels default back to `MonoBehaviourManager.UseAsyncLoop`.

# Theme System ThemeCache Refactoring **Related files:** > - `Src/Generators/VeloxDev.Core.Generator/Theme.cs` (+170 / -171, major rewrite)> - `Src/Core/VeloxDev.Core/DynamicTheme/ThemeCache.cs` (**Newly added**) > **Change amount:** +170 / -171 lines --- ## Background Previously, each class with `[ThemeConfig]` would generate a large number of static dictionary fields: ``` __velox__Converters__ — Converter instance dictionary __velox_Theme__Props__ — Property name → PropertyInfo dictionary __velox__Def__ThemeCache__ — Three-level nested dictionary (property name → PropertyInfo → theme type → value) __velox__Act__ThemeCache__ — Instance-level active override dictionary ``` This results in: - Each topic class generates large amounts of duplicate static field code - Does not support inheritance chain property merging. - Method cannot be overridden（`public` not `virtual`） ## Design Improvement Delegate all theme data uniformly to the centralized cache class `ThemeCache`, and the generated class only contains method implementations. ### ThemeCache Class Design **Namespace:** `VeloxDev.DynamicTheme` | Component | Description | | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | `_staticCache` | `Dictionary>` — Type-level static configuration | | `_activeCache` | `ConditionalWeakTable` — Instance-level active overrides | | `_converters` | `Dictionary` — Global converter instance pool | **Core API:** ```csharp public static class ThemeCache { // Type registration public static bool IsTypeRegistered(Type type); public static void RegisterType(Type type, Dictionary> properties); // Static cache query (inheritance chain merge) public static Dictionary GetStaticForType(Type type); // Instance-level active cache public static InstanceCache GetOrCreateActiveEntry(IThemeObject instance); public static InstanceCache? TryGetActiveEntry(IThemeObject instance); public static void RemoveActiveEntry(IThemeObject instance); // Default value lookup (inheritance chain traversal) public static bool TryGetDefaultValue(Type type, string propertyName, Type themeType, out object? value); } ``` ### Inheritance chain support `GetStaticForType(Type)` Merges properties by recursively traversing base classes: ```mermaid flowchart TD A[GetStaticForType(DerivedClass)] --> B[Recursively CollectStaticForType] B --> C[Process BaseType first] C --> D[Then process DerivedClass] D --> E[Derived class properties override base class properties with same name] E --> F[Return merged dictionary] ``` ### Method Overridability | Condition | Generated method modifier | | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------- | | Class is `sealed` | `public` (no modifier) | | Base class already has `[ThemeConfig]` (i.e., processed by generator) | `public override` | | Base class does not have `[ThemeConfig]` or no base class | `public virtual` | ### Lazy Registration ```mermaid flowchart LR A[InitializeTheme Call] --> B{IsTypeRegistered?} B -->|No| C[RegisterType Register Configuration] B -->|Yes| D[Skip Registration] C --> E[base.InitializeTheme
If base class exists] D --> E E --> F[ThemeManager.Register] F --> G[Iterate staticCache Apply Current Theme] ``` --- ## Converter Handling Changes | Aspect | v5.4.0 | v5.5.1 | | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | | Converter Instance | Each class generates a `static readonly` field | Each call uses inline `Activator.CreateInstance` | | Registration Method | Created in static initializer | Can be globally registered via `ThemeCache.RegisterConverter()` | --- ## Generator code quality improvement - Introduce auxiliary variables such as `classFullTypeName`, `methodModifier`, `baseCallInit` for unified processing. - Configuration collection no longer uses complex nested loops, changed to `configRegistrations` list. - `UpdatePropertyToCurrentTheme` reduced from 30+ lines to about 15 lines --- ## Backward Compatibility All public API signatures remain unchanged. - `SetThemeValue(string, object?)` - `RestoreThemeValue(string)` - `GetStaticThemeCache()` / `GetActiveThemeCache()` - `UpdatePropertyToCurrentTheme(string)` `UpdateAllPropertiesToCurrentTheme()` - `InitializeTheme()` - `ExecuteThemeChanging(Type?, Type?)` / `ExecuteThemeChanged(Type?, Type?)` But the behavior has subtle changes (see [API Usage Differences Comparison](../06-API使用差异对照/index.md)).

Focus on practical application and master how to use the project.
VeloxDev provides distinctive ViewModel completion and Command generation, with compatibility for mainstream MVVM frameworks in the community, avoiding redundant generation of infrastructure.

Children.Code: [VeloxProperty] private string name = string.Empty;
[VeloxProperty] public partial string Name { get; protected set; }

Children.Text: If a member derives from INotifyCollectionChanged, some additional callback functions are provided. Specifically, when the value of a notified property changes, the subscription and destruction of callback functions are handled internally. It is worth noting that partial properties cannot declare initial values at definition, so it is recommended to initialize these collections during the construction phase.
The following code will ultimately generate SaveCommand, regardless of whether it has the "Async" suffix.

No matter how you execute a command, the essence is waiting for a Task, so in most cases you don't need to worry that calling Execute instead of ExecuteAsync will block the UI; they are almost equivalent.

Children.Code:
SaveCommand.Execute(null);

await SaveCommand.ExecuteAsync(null);

Children.Text: The cancellation of the command also provides two versions; here, the waitable version is demonstrated.
When you set canValidate: true, a CanExecuteSaveCommand callback is generated internally. You can determine whether to allow the command execution in the callback function.

You can manually notify the UI to re-evaluate the executability of a command, at which point the parameter is null. When the command is executed, the parameter is the argument received by the command.

Children.Code: SaveCommand.Notify();

Children.Text: You can monitor the total number of currently executing tasks, perform exception handling, and other operations. These callback events all carry a CommandEventArgs parameter.
💡V5.4.0 has undergone API changes. Please go to the 【Version】 section to view.

.NET5+ natively supports proxy-based aspect-oriented programming, which means that without modifying the type source code, it can dynamically intercept and customize the behavior of properties and methods. VeloxDev provides source code generation for it.
There are five key parameters when setting up a proxy.
Use these parameters in the SetProxy method.

Cells: Parameters received at the entry point, such as the value keyword in properties, parameters obtained when calling a method.
Parameters returned by the previous lifecycle

Children.Text: The hook will only take effect when properties are accessed through a proxy. In actual business, you can choose as needed.
Frame loop behavior is common in scenarios such as physics simulation and state monitoring. Now, you can build it using a trait.
You can create multiple groups based on the channel parameter, and you can use the fps parameter to specify the refresh rate.
Basic control can be implemented based on these static functions.

Children.Code:
MonoBehaviourManager.Start("game"); // Start

MonoBehaviourManager.Pause("game"); // Pause (not equal to stop)

MonoBehaviourManager.Resume("game"); // Resume

await MonoBehaviourManager.StopAsync("game"); // Stop

Children.Text: You can modify the time multiplier and refresh rate at any time.

Children.Code:
MonoBehaviourManager.SetTimeScale(0.5f, "game"); // Time scale

MonoBehaviourManager.SetTargetFPS(30, "game"); // Frame rate

bool isRun = MonoBehaviourManager.IsRunning("game")；

bool isPaused = MonoBehaviourManager.IsPaused("game")；

Cells: Time elapsed since the last frame

Children.Text: Almost any GUI framework has dedicated interfaces for handling animations, which means that the creation of interpolation animations depends to some extent on the GUI's syntax design and may have limitations. Taking WPF as an example: standard animations must be built based on DependencyProperty, which, while beneficial for internal performance optimization, also sacrifices flexibility.

VeloxDev advocates compatibility, with a fully internal abstraction layer for animations from construction to execution. Each GUI framework requires only a small amount of adaptation code, and the final animation acts on standard properties rather than any GUI-specific objects.
Theme - Implementing Dynamic Theme Color Switching
You can use the same API to build animations in any supported GUI framework. We provide the most elegant approach to date, with a focus on type safety and thread safety.
In the following example, you will create an animation to modify the horizontal offset and fill color of a Rectangle element.
You can split the animation process into multiple segments, then link them together in sequence.

Children.Code:
```csharp
Transition<Rectangle>.Create()
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .Then()  // Link, immediately execute the next animation
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .AwaitThen() // Link, wait for a specific time before executing the next animation
    .Property()
    .Effect();
```

Children.Text: Use the following code to control the animation toggle.

Children.Code:
animation.Execute(rect); // start, animation acts on rect instance

Transition.Exit(rect); // terminate

Children.Text: The startup of animations is thread-safe, so there is no need to worry about the following scenarios.

The IInterpolable interface allows types to directly participate in interpolation. By implementing this interface, when you build animations in Fluent, the Property function can directly use custom types
IEaseCalculator interface allows you to customize easing effects

Animation is based on interpolating between two states. The snapshot mechanism is one of its implementations. VeloxDev has exposed it, so you can also use this mechanism to create animations, or for state saving and restoration (only used in animation scenarios).

Children.Code:
// Essentially an animation, configurable effects
snapshot.Effect(TransitionEffects.Empty).Execute(view);

Children.Text: This is a branch capability extended from the animation system, which can naturally transition from one theme color scheme to another in the UI.
If you configure an interpolator, the following functions will become available.
Of course, considering that gradient-based transitions may cause performance issues in some scenarios, a method without loading animations is also provided and is supported at all times.

Process-oriented and automation are common in modern software, especially in business pipelines and device control flows. Now, you can elegantly build easily extensible runtime workflows in .NET. Moreover, with the development of artificial intelligence, it is no longer far off for intelligent agents to understand and manipulate in-memory objects. What you need is creativity and approval, not a large amount of code writing.
Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI / Winforms have all implemented the following effect.
Based on MVVM, without any GUI dependencies

You can draw an interactive workflow orchestration view in your preferred GUI framework, or run the orchestrated workflow on a server without a UI. VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides persistence services to support sharing the same data across these modes.
Implement spatial indexing and querying of nodes based on grid hash algorithm, achieving microsecond-level response in actual measurements.
The workflow framework has deeply integrated the Function Calling capability provided by MAF, so your intelligent agent can now understand and manipulate workflows at runtime.
WorkflowBuilder provides source code generation capability; by simply annotating this feature, the workflow component kernel can be implemented without inheritance.

WorkflowHelper provides the logic code required by workflow components. They are injected into the view model during the generation phase and support dynamic replacement, achieving a highly extensible architecture by overriding built-in functions of Helper and subscribing to built-in events of Helper.
A workflow is a graph structure that contains four important components. This chapter will show you how to build the view models corresponding to the components.

Cells: Global container that stores all data of a single workflow.
Node that carries configuration and business execution.
Connector that carries connection relationships and channel direction restrictions.
Connection line that can access the Slot of the start and end points.

Children.Text: Next, all view models will be built based on the following annotated features. $component is the component type, $helper is the injected business logic.

Children.Code: 【WorkflowBuilder.{$component}<{$helper}>】

Children.Text:
| Member                                             | Type                                                                                               | Description                   |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `VirtualLink`                                    | `IWorkflowLinkViewModel`                                                                         | Virtual connection during connection establishment |
| `Nodes`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowNodeViewModel>`                                                   | All Node components           |
| `Links`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowLinkViewModel>`                                                   | All Link components           |
| `Layout`                                          | 'CanvasLayout'                                                                                                            | Canvas layout                 |

| Member                                              | Type                                                                                               | Description                |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| `CreateNodeCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Create node command        |
| `SetPointerCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Update pointer position command |
| `ResetVirtualLinkCommand`                        | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Reset virtual link command |
| `RedoCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Redo command               |
| `UndoCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Undo command               |

In the definition phase, the node view model has two special members:
- IWorkflowSlotViewModel derived classes must be marked with [VeloxProperty]
- SlotEnumerator<T> can use bool or enum as conditional branches, and internally generates multiple Slots based on the conditions; it must also be marked with [VeloxProperty]

In this way, you can directly render multiple connectors from the InputSlot property and OutputSlots collection in the view. Note that the UI is responsible for tracking the coordinates (Anchor) of the slots and passing them back to the view model. If the UI lacks such coordinate tracking, the start and end positions of your Link component cannot be calculated correctly.

SlotEnumerator<T> implements the IEnumerable interface, and its child elements are defined as follows: Name is the name of the enumeration member, Value is the enumeration value, and Slot is the automatically constructed Slot component.

| 成员                                               | 类型                                             | 说明                 |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------- |
| `Parent`                                         | `IWorkflowTreeViewModel?`                      | Parent Tree            |
| `Anchor`                                         | `Anchor`                                       | Node anchor coordinates         |
| `Size`                                           | `Size`                                         | Node size             |
| `Slots`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Collection of slots owned by the node |

| Member                                               | Type                                             | Description                |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------- |
| `MoveCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Move node command          |
| `SetAnchorCommand`                               | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Set anchor command         |
| `SetSizeCommand`                                 | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Set size command           |
| `CreateSlotCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Create slot command        |
| `DeleteCommand`                                  | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Delete node command        |
| `WorkCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Execute work command       |
| `BroadcastCommand`                               | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Forward broadcast command  |
| `ReverseBroadcastCommand`                        | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Reverse broadcast command  |

| Member                                               | Type                                             | Description                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `Targets`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Collection of targets currently pointed to by the Slot |
| `Sources`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Collection of sources currently received by the Slot |
| `Parent`                                           | `IWorkflowNodeViewModel?`                      | Parent Node                                       |
| `Channel`                                          | `SlotChannel`                                  | Slot channel type                                 |
| `State`                                            | `SlotState`                                    | Slot connection state                             |
| `Anchor`                                           | `Anchor`                                       | Slot anchor position                              |

| Member                                               | Type                                             | Description                      |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `SetChannelCommand`                                  | `IVeloxCommand`                                  | Set channel command              |
| `SendConnectionCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                  | Send connection command          |
| `ReceiveConnectionCommand`                           | `IVeloxCommand`                                  | Receive connection command       |
| `DeleteCommand`                                      | `IVeloxCommand`                                  | Delete Slot command              |

| Member | Type | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----------- |
| `Sender` | `IWorkflowSlotViewModel` | Connection initiator |
| `Receiver` | `IWorkflowSlotViewModel` | Connection receiver |
| `IsVisible` | `bool` | Whether the connection is visible |

The logic of all components in a workflow is injectable and replaceable, and provides events and overridable methods for you to customize. Each workflow component you define will hold a Helper property, based on which you can access the business implementation.

When using the generic version, all built-in Component properties of Helper will degenerate into specific derived classes, while the Component properties inside the non-generic version are abstract interface forms.

Cells: Provides extended behavior for the global container

Children.Text:
| Member                                                                               | Type                                          | Description             |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| `Install(IWorkflowTreeViewModel tree)`                                               | `void`                                        | Install to Tree         |
| `Uninstall(IWorkflowTreeViewModel tree)`                                             | `void`                                        | Uninstall from Tree     |
| `CreateNode(IWorkflowNodeViewModel node)`                                            | `void`                                        | Create node             |
| `CreateLink(IWorkflowSlotViewModel sender, IWorkflowSlotViewModel receiver)`         | `IWorkflowLinkViewModel`                      | Create link             |
| `SetPointer(Anchor anchor)`                                                          | `void`                                        | Set pointer position    |
| `ValidateConnection(IWorkflowSlotViewModel sender, IWorkflowSlotViewModel receiver)` | `bool`                                        | Validate connection     |
| `SendConnection(IWorkflowSlotViewModel slot)`                                        | `void`                                        | Initiate connection     |
| `ReceiveConnection(IWorkflowSlotViewModel slot)`                                     | `void`                                        | Receive connection      |
| `ResetVirtualLink()`                                                                 | `void`                                        | Reset virtual link      |
| `Virtualize(Viewport viewport)`                                                      | `void`                                        | Trigger viewport virtualization |
| `Submit(IWorkflowActionPair actionPair)`                                             | `void`                                        | Submit action           |
| `Redo()`                                                                             | `void`                                        | Redo                    |
| `Undo()`                                                                             | `void`                                        | Undo                    |
| `ClearHistory()`                                                                     | `void`                                        | Clear history           |

VeloxDev has provided item template support for some platforms that support additional behaviors. Using them can efficiently build workflow views. This chapter demonstrates basic command-line usage and points for fine-tuning template code. Details about additional properties can be found in the next section.
Installation - {$plateform} optional Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI

Children.Code: dotnet new install VeloxDev.{$plateform}.Templates

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} Options: ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-tree -n TreeView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: For the item template of the Tree component, it is often necessary to modify the ViewModel → View mapping. Generally, for Avalonia, there is no need to write a template selector, while other platforms must create one.

Children.Code:
```xml
<!-- VeloxDev customization: Generate the Node, Slot, Link, selector, and grid-decorator templates, then update the local type names below if you renamed them. -->
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
			 xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
			 xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008"
			 xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006"
			 xmlns:local="using:TemplateNamespace"
			 mc:Ignorable="d" d:DesignWidth="800" d:DesignHeight="450"
			 x:Class="TemplateNamespace.TemplateClass"
			 xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors"
			 xmlns:framework="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.IsEnabled="True" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.ScrollViewerName="PART_ScrollViewer" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.CanvasName="PART_Canvas" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.GridDecoratorName="PART_GridDecorator" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.PointerPressSourceName="PART_SurfaceBorder"
			 x:CompileBindings="False">
			 <!-- Replace x:CompileBindings=False with a concrete x:DataType after defining your ViewModel. -->
	
	<UserControl.DataTemplates>
		<!-- Node Templates -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="framework:IWorkflowNodeViewModel">
			<local:NodeView Width="{Binding Size.Width}" Height="{Binding Size.Height}" Canvas.Left="{Binding Anchor.Horizontal}" Canvas.Top="{Binding Anchor.Vertical}" ZIndex="{Binding Anchor.Layer}" RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:TemplateClass].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}" />
		</DataTemplate>

		<!-- Connection Templates -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="framework:IWorkflowLinkViewModel">
			<local:LinkView StartLeft="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Horizontal}" StartTop="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Vertical}" EndLeft="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Horizontal}" EndTop="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Vertical}" ZIndex="-1" CanRender="{Binding IsVisible}" Width="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Width}" Height="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Height}" RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:TemplateClass].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}" />
		</DataTemplate>
	</UserControl.DataTemplates>

</UserControl>
```

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} Optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-node -n NodeView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: For the item template of the Node component, it is often necessary to modify the rendering method of its internal Slot members. As mentioned in previous documentation, the view model of the Node component can define a single Slot or a collection of multiple Slots in two ways. In XAML, they need to pass their Name to the corresponding attached properties, namely SlotNames and SlotEnumeratorNames, both of which can separate multiple members with commas.

Children.Code:
<!-- Customize the content, but keep PART_* names synchronized with WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior. -->
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
			 xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
			 xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008"
			 xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006"
			 xmlns:local="using:TemplateNamespace"
			 xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors"
			 mc:Ignorable="d" d:DesignWidth="800" d:DesignHeight="450"
			 x:Class="TemplateNamespace.TemplateClass"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.IsEnabled="True" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.SlotNames="PART_InputSlot" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.SlotEnumeratorNames="PART_OutputSlots" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.CoordinateHostName="PART_Canvas"
			 Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground"
			 x:CompileBindings="False">
			 <!-- Replace x:CompileBindings=False with a concrete x:DataType after defining your ViewModel. -->

        <!-- Node drag behavior -->
		<Grid behaviors:WorkflowNodeDragBehavior.CoordinateHostName="PART_Canvas" behaviors:WorkflowNodeDragBehavior.IsEnabled="True"
			  Grid.Row="0"
			  Margin="12,0">
		</Grid>
        
        <!-- Single Slot component -->
		<local:SlotView x:Name="PART_InputSlot" DataContext="{Binding InputSlot}"
						Width="18"
						Height="18"
						Margin="2,0,0,0"
						HorizontalAlignment="Left"
						VerticalAlignment="Center"
						Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground" />
     
        <!-- Multiple Slot components -->
		<ItemsControl x:Name="PART_OutputSlots" ItemsSource="{Binding OutputSlots.Items}"
					  Grid.Row="1"
					  Margin="12,6"
					  VerticalAlignment="Center">
			<ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
				<DataTemplate>
					<Grid ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto" Margin="0,3" ClipToBounds="False">
						<TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}"
								   Margin="0,0,10,0"
								   HorizontalAlignment="Right"
								   VerticalAlignment="Center" />
						<local:SlotView DataContext="{Binding Slot}"
												Grid.Column="1"
												Width="14"
												Height="14"
												Margin="0"
												Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground" />
					</Grid>
				</DataTemplate>
			</ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
		</ItemsControl>

</UserControl>

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-slot -n SlotView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} available ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-link -n LinkView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-decorator -n GridDecorator -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: The grid decorator communicates data with the adapter based on the following interface. When users interact, the four properties here will be updated as necessary. Only the drawing logic of the decorator itself needs to be considered.
Create - {$plateform} optional ava / wpf / winui / maui, among which ava can skip the use of this template

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-selector -n ComponentSelector -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: If you have already read Component Building and Business Logic Injection, then now, the workflow is ready to run.
The problem solved in this section is: how to implement workflow GUI based on additional behaviors.

This chapter applies to Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI (Winforms does not have an attached property mechanism, only the API is similar; the recommended approach is to directly port from the Demo in the repository; Razor is still in experimental stage).

Children.Code: <UserControl xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors" />

Children.Text: Provide behavior registration based on Name capture at the UserControl level, covering the interactive behaviors required by elements such as Canvas, ScrollViewer, and grid decoration lines.

| Additional Properties | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `IsEnabled` | `bool` | Enable interaction behavior for the entire workflow canvas |
| `ScrollViewerName` | `string?` | Specify the `ScrollViewer` used for scrolling and viewport calculations |
| `CanvasName` | `string?` | Specify the workflow content canvas |
| `GridDecoratorName` | `string?` | Specify the grid/ruler decorator used to synchronize scroll offset and content offset |
| `PointerPressSourceName` | `string?` | Specify the element that receives press events to initiate canvas panning |
Providing an object pool mechanism for Canvas, reusing view elements plays a key role in reducing GC pressure and improving rendering performance.

Cells: Depends on the template selector interface of the GUI framework

Children.Text: Node mount connector, this set of additional properties is responsible for the behavior of these connectors.

Cells: Slot needs to be laid out in a host container
Performs offset coordinate detection based on a host container during drag

Children.Text: Responsible for injecting the behavior of initiating and receiving connections into the connector.
No additional attributes, depends on control implementation.
Enable LLM to understand your workflow and automatically build, configure, execute, and approve (automatic / interactive)
💡API changes have occurred in V5.1.1, you can go to the 【Version】 section to view.

VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides a set of extension methods for view models with the following features:  
- `Dictionary<,>` can use any type as Key, no longer limited to `string`  
- Only when both the setter and getter of a property are `public` will that property be serialized, and serialization only handles properties

| Goal/Scenario | Configuration | Rationale | Recommended? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Debugging, viewing JSON, diffing | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Best JSON readability, and fully preserves polymorphism, null values, and default values, most suitable for troubleshooting workflow restoration issues. | Highly recommended |
| Formally saving to file | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Smaller file, but retains all information needed for restoration; suitable for workflow persistence. | Recommended |
| Pursuing minimum size only | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Ignore).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Ignore)` | Reduces JSON size, but may lose fields required for restoration; not suitable for complex workflow object graphs. | Use with caution |
| With derived nodes, interface properties, polymorphic objects | `WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto)` | Allows deserialization to restore actual runtime types; typically required in workflow scenarios. | Mandatory |
| Expecting state to be fully consistent after restoration | `WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Preserves null and default values to avoid state discrepancies after deserialization. | Recommended |
| Simply want JSON to be more readable | `WithIndented()` | Only improves readability; does not change object semantics. | As needed |
| Simply want to reduce file size | `WithCompact()` | Removes indentation and whitespace; simplest compression method. | As needed |
| Using synchronous API | `var json = tree.Serialize(options);` / `var tree = json.Deserialize<TreeViewModel>(options);` | Suitable for simple save/load. | Recommended |
| Safe loading | `json.TryDeserialize<TreeViewModel>(options, out var tree)` | More robust when external files or user input may be corrupted; does not throw exceptions directly. | Highly recommended |
| Using asynchronous API | `await tree.SerializeAsync(options)` / `await json.DeserializeAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for UI, commands, file IO pipelines. | Recommended |
| Using stream API | `await tree.SerializeToStreamAsync(stream, options)` / `await stream.DeserializeFromStreamAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for file streams, network streams, memory streams. | Recommended |
| Most reliable default workflow configuration | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | If you are unsure which to choose, use this set; it is the safest for workflow object graphs. | Default preference |
This chapter continuously tracks the usage of VeloxDev in different GUI frameworks, collecting and answering questions.
To ensure thread safety, when using VeloxDev.WinUI, you must let UIThreadInspector obtain the window.

The serialization dependency reflection provided by VeloxDev.Core.Extension, and some capabilities of the agent depend on serialization. If you encounter an inability to run the Agent or perform serialization in a trimming-sensitive environment, you can try disabling trimming.

Children.Code: <PublishTrimmed>false</PublishTrimmed>

Children.Text: Since not all runtimes support Thread, the frame loop takes two paths. To be conservative, versions lower than .NET5 default to async instead of Thread, while higher versions have built-in platform detection, and the detection logic follows the table below.

MonoBehaviourManager has a static property named UseAsyncLoop. If true, asynchronous mode is enabled for maximum compatibility. You can modify UseAsyncLoop before executing the Start() function provided by this class.

Check the mounting order of your components: The Node component must be mounted to the Tree component before internal commands can be executed, and the Slot component must be mounted to the Node component before internal commands can be executed.
As part of our plan, we hope to collect some good practices. Any interesting projects you create with VeloxDev can be submitted to the following email address.
